mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a monetary policy tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing starts above 1.2 million units over 3 months signals an increase in supply-side capacity that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a monetary policy easing cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% over 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment below 130,000 units over 2 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining payroll employment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a monetary policy easing cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 16000 over 6 months signals a strengthening demand environment that increases corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a monetary policy tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the consumer price index below 230 over 2 months signals a weakening inflation environment that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.
